policy_statement: Egypt’s sustainable energy policy roadmap targets 22% of total electricity consumption from renewables by 2022 and 80% of electricity demand from local generation by 2020.
verbatim_text:
Egypt developed its sustainable energy policy road map with the aim to increase the operating and technical efficiency of distribution utilities, improve energy conservation and load management and diversify the sources of the regional electricity supply.

The goals of this roadmap are three folds:

1. 22% of total electricity consumption sourced from renewables by 2022.

... 

4. 80% of electricity demand from local generation (of all fuels) by 2020.

The text frames them as “goals of this roadmap,” which is more target-setting than an explicit legal mandate, so binding mechanism is not evidenced, but weak language (“aim”) is mitigated by the presence of hard quantified targets and dates.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: The text specifies measurable shares: "22% of total electricity consumption sourced from renewables" and "80% of electricity demand from local generation". Timeline: Each target includes a deadline: "by 2022" and "by 2020". Binding mechanism: No explicit enforceable instrument is cited (no law/mandate/requirement language), only that Egypt "developed" a roadmap with "goals" and an "aim". Spatial specificity: The policy is national in scope, indicated by "Egypt developed..." and references to Egypt’s electricity system. Weak language: "with the aim to" indicates aspirational framing. Strong language: Despite the 'aim' phrasing, the roadmap enumerates hard numeric goals with deadlines (22% by 2022; 80% by 2020), which are auditable outcome targets. policy_statement: Egypt’s Energy Efficiency Plan in the Electricity Sector targeted savings of 5,566 GWh between 2012 and 2015 via efficient household lighting (distribution of 60 million bulbs), appliance standards and labelling, and street-lighting energy savings (1,200 GWh).

Egypt has developed an Energy Efficiency Plan in the Electricity Sector saving 5,566 GWH between 2012 and 2015, mainly by measures in the lighting sector, including:

- -The first phase: Change to high efficiency lighting in the household sector. The planned distribution of 60 million bulbs shall save 3,320 GWH,
- -The second phase: programme of energy efficiency standards and labelling for household appliances. This shall save 1,663 GWH, and
- -Energy saving in street lighting of 1,200 GWH; supported by the Association of Energy Efficiency Engineers (AEEE).
